text: Developing a mobile app requires careful planning, especially regarding responsive design. This means the app must adapt seamlessly to different screen sizes and orientations, whether on a phone or tablet. A key decision is choosing between native and cross-platform development. Native apps are built for a specific operating system, offering higher performance, while cross-platform tools allow one codebase to run on both iOS and Android. After development, the app is submitted to app stores like Google Play or the Apple App Store for review and distribution. Performance is crucial: users expect fast loading times, smooth navigation, and minimal battery drain to keep them engaged.
